script:
It is early.

You said you would ship the OATH v1 spec to GitHub and send Oliver the link before noon.

That was last night. That was a promise.

Kobe was in the gym at 4am. Not to be seen. Not to be praised. He got there before the building was warm, before anyone else had a reason to be awake, and he put up 800 shots. The work existed whether or not it was witnessed.

This is the same.

Trust the work.

Open the spec doc. Write the wedge section. That is the only thing that exists right now — not the full document, not Oliver's inbox, not the clock. The wedge section.

Forty-five minutes. Phone face down.

Write.

attempts.user_prompt_used:
Generate the OATH ritual script using:

mode: hardest_work
variant: more_narrative
user's intent (the hardest task they swore last night they'd tackle this morning): "ship the OATH v1 spec to github and send Oliver the link before noon"
user's first action (what they'll do in the first 5 minutes): "open the spec doc and write the wedge section"
user's hero (reference by concrete habit, never by invented quote): Kobe Bryant
user's grounding phrase (use verbatim, do not paraphrase): "trust the work"

Target read duration: 60 seconds (~150 words at 150 wpm).

Output as JSON:
{
  "script": "the spoken text, with line breaks for natural pauses",
  "estimated_duration_seconds": <integer>,
  "word_count": <integer>
}

Output the JSON only. No preamble. No explanation. No markdown fence.
